blocks.content: Friday. Final day of tryouts. The list goes up Monday morning.

blocks.speaker: Coach Briggs
blocks.content: Yesterday told me who has speed. Today tells me who has sense. A fast player with no football sense is just a fast person on a football field. That's not useful to me.
He ran us through situational drills. Route recognition. Reading the defense. What do you do when the coverage changes after the snap?
I ran it. Got the ball. But I ran it in the wrong direction — I read the coverage late and adjusted when I should have committed.

blocks.speaker: Coach Briggs
blocks.content: Again. Same route. This time you're reading before the snap, not after.
I ran it again. Got it right.

blocks.speaker: Coach Briggs
blocks.content: There it is. You can learn. That matters more than speed.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Bruh Coach just said you can learn. That's a compliment. He doesn't say that.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: How do you know he doesn't say that?

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Because I've been watching him for forty-five minutes and that's the first time he said anything that wasn't a correction.
